title: Distephanus mangokensis
taxonomy: Distephanus mangokensis is a species within the plant family Asteraceae. It is a part of the tribe Inuleae and is closely related to other species in the same family. The genus Distephanus belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae. Distephanus mangokensis is native to the island of Madagascar. It is one of the many flowering plants found in the tropical regions of the island.

intro: Distephanus mangokensis is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of D. mangokensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often in combination with other herbs, to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
